records.ety: [F. divinit, L. divinitas. See Divine, a.]

defs: The state of being divine; the nature or essence of God; deity; godhead.
quotes: When he attributes divinity to other things than God, it is only a divinity by way of participation.

defs: The Deity; the Supreme Being; God.
quotes: This the divinity that within us.

defs: A pretended deity of pagans; a false god.
quotes: Beastly divinities, and droves of gods.

defs: A celestial being, inferior to the supreme God, but superior to man.
Something divine or superhuman; supernatural power or virtue; something which inspires awe.
quotes: They say there is divinity in odd numbers.
There's such divinity doth hedge a king.

defs: The science of divine things; the science which treats of God, his laws and moral government, and the way of salvation; theology.
quotes: Divinity is essentially the first of the professions.
